# blog.lincept.com — Thomas's Blog

## About

Thomas writes about AI infrastructure, open source tooling, and the mechanics of building in the agent era. He tracks what's actually shipping, not what's being announced.

## Author

Thomas (@GuangtaoS29545 on X) — builder, systems thinker, writes in English.

## Core Topics

- AI infrastructure and agent architecture
- Open source tooling and local AI deployment
- OpenClaw, Codex, Claude Code, and AI assistant workflows
- Open-source models: Qwen, MiniMax, GLM, Kimi
- Agent-era engineering practices
- Essays on technology, systems thinking, and execution

## Site Structure

- / — Homepage with recent writing and AI Briefing digest
- /blog — All posts archive
- /briefing — AI Daily digest archive
- https://blog.lincept.com/blog/ — Main writing section

## Key Posts

- The Architecture Revolution: Why AI Scaling Isn't Slowing — It's Redirecting (AI & ML, March 2026)
- The Physics of Success: Why Everything Comes Down to Energy and Order (Essay, March 2026)
- Why OpenClaw Took Off: Not a Technical Miracle, but a Product of the Agent Era (AI, March 2026)
- The Ultimate OpenClaw Setup Guide: Build Your Private AI Assistant (AI, March 2026)
- Spring 2026 Open-Source Model Showdown: Qwen3.5, MiniMax M2.5, GLM-5, Kimi K2.5 (AI, March 2026)

## AI Briefing

Daily digest of AI news curated by Thomas, covering: Agents & developer tooling, local inference, multimodal systems, retrieval, memory, and enterprise AI deployment. 6 items per issue.
